audit_status.source_base: Greek Romans text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for weak/strong, food/days, nonjudgment, Lordship, conscience, kingdom as justice/peace/joy, not causing stumbling.

verses.restored: Receive the one weak in trust, not into disputes of reasonings.
One trusts to eat all things, but the weak one eats vegetables.
Let the one eating not despise the one not eating, and let the one not eating not judge the one eating, for the Divine received that one.
Who are you judging another's household servant? To one's own lord one stands or falls; and that one will be made to stand, for the Lord is powerful to make that one stand.
One judges one day above another; another judges every day alike. Let each be fully assured in one's own mind.
The one minding the day minds it to the Lord; and the one eating eats to the Lord, for that one gives thanks to the Divine; and the one not eating abstains to the Lord and gives thanks to the Divine.
For none of us lives to oneself, and none dies to oneself.
For if we live, we live to the Lord; and if we die, we die to the Lord. Therefore whether we live or die, we are the Lord's.
For to this Messiah died and lived, so that he might be Lord of both dead and living.
But you, why do you judge your sibling? Or you also, why do you despise your sibling? For we will all stand before the judgment seat of the Divine.
For it has been written: As I live, says the Lord, every knee will bend to me, and every tongue will confess to the Divine.
So then each of us will give account concerning oneself to the Divine.
Therefore let us no longer judge one another, but judge this rather: not to place stumbling block or snare before the sibling.
I know and am persuaded in Lord Jesus that nothing is common through itself, except to the one calculating something common, to that one it is common.
For if because of food your sibling is grieved, you no longer walk according to love. Do not destroy with your food the one for whom Messiah died.
Therefore do not let your good be spoken of as harmful.
For the reign of the Divine is not eating and drinking, but righteousness/justice and peace and joy in holy Spirit.
For the one serving Messiah in this is pleasing to the Divine and approved by humans.
So then let us pursue the things of peace and the things of building up one another.
Do not tear down the work of the Divine because of food. All things indeed are clean, but it is harmful for a human to eat through stumbling.
It is beautiful/good not to eat flesh nor drink wine nor do anything by which your sibling stumbles.
The trust that you have, have according to yourself before the Divine. Blessed/flourishing is the one not judging oneself in what one approves.
But the one doubting, if eating, has been condemned, because it is not from trust; and everything that is not from trust is sin/misalignment.

translation_notes: Romans 14 is about conscience, table fellowship, and non-destruction of siblings, not universalizing one group's scruples.
Koinos/common language is preserved carefully: Paul says nothing is common in itself, yet conscience matters.
